description_long: This structure is a stone pagoda with a hexagonal base, rising in a series of receding, tiered levels that culminate in a tall, slender spire. The base is broad and stepped, providing a solid foundation, while the body of the pagoda features intricately carved arches and shallow reliefs on each face. The roof sections are stacked in a pyramidal fashion, each tier slightly smaller than the one below, creating a visually striking, layered effect. The spire at the top is elongated and finely detailed, resembling a traditional stupa finial. The entire structure is rendered in a uniform, sandy stone color, giving it a monolithic and ancient appearance. The surface texture appears rough and weathered, consistent with traditional stone masonry.
description: A multi-tiered stone pagoda with a hexagonal base, ornate arches, and a tall, pointed spire, all in a sandy stone color.
description_view_0: Front view showing the hexagonal base, central arches, and the full height of the spire.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more of the side arches and the stepped base.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with clear visibility of the layered roof tiers and spire.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the symmetry of the arches and the receding roof levels.
